task_completion.description: Verify that a horizontal and a vertical line segment forming an approximate 90-degree corner are drawn.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two straight line segments, one roughly horizontal and one roughly vertical, forming an L-shape on the SolveSpace canvas.

task_completion.description: Verify that an arc (fillet) is added at the inside corner of the lines.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A curved line segment (arc) is visible connecting the inside corner of the two straight lines.

task_completion.description: Verify that the arc is constrained to be tangent to both line segments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The transition between the straight lines and the arc is perfectly smooth with no sharp angles. SolveSpace tangent constraint icons (a line touching a circle) should be visible at the junction points.

task_completion.description: Verify that a dimension constraint is applied to the arc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension line with text is attached to the arc.

task_completion.description: Verify that the arc dimension is exactly 20mm (diameter) or 10mm (radius).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The dimension text on the arc reads '20' (if measuring diameter) or '10' (if measuring radius). It may include prefixes like 'd' or 'r'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the file is saved as fillet_profile.slvs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace window title bar displays 'fillet_profile.slvs' or a save dialog confirms the file is being saved to '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/fillet_profile.slvs'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the sketch.
integrity.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace application window is visible with its toolbars, property panels, and canvas, rather than just a terminal or text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the canvas to draw the shapes step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: Progressive drawing steps (e.g., drawing lines first, then adding the arc, then applying constraints and dimensions) are visible across the screenshot history.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the GUI by running a script to generate the .slvs file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the agent actively selecting tools (line, arc, dimension) and clicking on the SolveSpace canvas.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file name is 'fillet_profile.slvs'. The arc must have a diameter of exactly 20.0 mm (which is equivalent to a radius of 10.0 mm). The tolerance for the dimension is 0.5 mm. The agent must draw an arc (not a full circle) and apply tangent constraints between the arc and both lines. Note: The programmatic verifier has bugs regarding arc and tangent constraint types, but visually you should strictly look for an actual arc with tangent constraints and the correct 20mm diameter / 10mm radius dimension.
